items.definition_en: A flash flood is a sudden and strong flood that happens quickly after heavy rain.
items.example_en: The village was destroyed by a flash flood last night.

items.definition_en: A hailstorm is a storm with a lot of hail falling from the sky.
items.example_en: Cars were damaged during last night's hailstorm.

items.definition_en: An ice jam is a build-up of ice in a river that blocks the flow of water.
items.example_en: The ice jam caused the river to flood nearby streets.

items.definition_en: A polar vortex is a large pocket of very cold air over the polar regions.
items.example_en: The polar vortex brought freezing temperatures to the city.

items.definition_en: A derecho is a long-lasting and powerful windstorm that moves in a straight line.
items.example_en: The derecho knocked down hundreds of trees in the area.

items.definition_en: A microburst is a sudden, strong, and brief downward wind.
items.example_en: The microburst damaged several roofs in our neighborhood.

items.definition_en: A supercell is a very powerful thunderstorm with a rotating core.
items.example_en: A supercell can cause large hail and tornadoes.

items.definition_en: Sea fog is a thick fog that forms over the sea and moves onto land.
items.example_en: The ship entered heavy sea fog at dawn.

items.definition_en: Ice fog occurs when tiny ice crystals form in the air in cold weather.
items.example_en: The village was covered in thick ice fog this morning.

items.definition_en: A river flood happens when a river overflows its banks.
items.example_en: Spring rains often cause a river flood in the valley.

items.definition_en: An ice floe is a large flat piece of floating ice.
items.example_en: Polar bears sometimes travel on an ice floe.

items.definition_en: Freezing fog is fog that forms when water droplets freeze on contact.
items.example_en: Driving became dangerous because of the freezing fog.

items.definition_en: A storm surge is a rise in sea level caused by a storm's winds.
items.example_en: The coastal town was flooded by a storm surge.

items.definition_en: Ice storm damage means harm caused to buildings or trees by ice storms.
items.example_en: Many trees fell due to ice storm damage.

items.definition_en: Lake-effect snow is heavy snow that falls when cold air moves over warm lake water.
items.example_en: The city receives a lot of lake-effect snow every winter.

items.definition_en: River ice is frozen water that covers the surface of a river.
items.example_en: Fishermen waited until the river ice was thick enough.

items.definition_en: Rainstorm damage refers to harm caused by heavy rainfall.
items.example_en: The roof collapsed from rainstorm damage.

items.definition_en: A storm warning is an official alert about an approaching storm.
items.example_en: The government issued a storm warning for the coast.

items.definition_en: Wind chill is what the temperature feels like because of the wind.
items.example_en: The wind chill made it feel much colder than it really was.

items.definition_en: The heat index shows how hot the weather feels when humidity is included.
items.example_en: The heat index reached dangerous levels today.
